records.stage_evidence: Clinical history mentions 'LT DJ STENT' and findings describe a 'well-positioned left double-J ureteral stent'.

ocr.verbatim:
PATIENT NAME : 
PROCEDURE DATE : 31/03/2026
AGE/SEX : 33 YEAR/MALE
REFERRING DR. :

X-RAY KUBS

CLINICAL HISTORY : LT DJ STENT

FIDINGS:
Visualized renal outlines are normal in size, shape,position and axis.
Well-positioned left double-J ureteral stent in the Left flank.

No radio-opaque calcitacaton is seen in renal areas, hepatobiliary region or pancreatic area.
Both psoas shadows are normal and bilaterally symmetrical.
No abnormal soft tissue mass seen.
Lumbosacral spine is normal.

IMPRESSION:
NO RADIO-OPAQUE RENAL STONES OR GALL STONES SEEN.
Well-positioned left double-J ureteral stent in the Left flank.

Please correlate clinically.

EVERY TEST HAS ITS OWN INHERENT LIMITATIONS, THEREFORE IN CASE OF ANY DISCREPANCE WITH CLINICAL PROFILE, PLEASE INFORM TO CENTRE IMMEDIATELY.
Timing : Monday to Saturday 08:00 AM to 08:00 PM, Sunday 8:00 AM to 04:00 PM
इमर्जेंसी सेवा 24 घंटे उपलब्ध है !
Not For Medico Legal Purpose

records.summary: This is a typed radiology report for an X-ray KUB (Kidney, Ureter, Bladder) performed on a 33-year-old male on March 31, 2026. The report confirms the presence of a well-positioned left double-J ureteral stent and notes the absence of radio-opaque renal or gall stones. The renal outlines, psoas shadows, and lumbosacral spine are described as normal.
key_observations: Patient is 33-year-old male, procedure date is 31/03/2026.
Left double-J ureteral stent is well-positioned in the left flank.
No radio-opaque renal or gall stones detected.
Renal outlines, psoas shadows, and lumbosacral spine are normal.
Report advises clinical correlation.
Contains disclaimer: 'Not For Medico Legal Purpose'.

records.summary: Post-procedure KUB X-ray showing a urinary catheter in the bladder of a 33-year-old male, dated March 31, 2026.
key_observations: A urinary catheter is visible, extending from the bladder to the perineum.
Spine and pelvic bones are clearly visualized.
Bladder appears distended with catheter tip within.
Image is labeled with patient age, gender, study type (KUB), and date.

records.stage_evidence: Report describes post-contrast findings (e.g., '5 mts after contrast injection') and includes impression and advice.

ocr.verbatim:
NAME OF PATIENT :
AGE SEX: 34Year/Male
REF BY DR: SELF
DATE: 21-03-2026
PART OF INVESTIGATION X-RAY IVP

X-RAY IVP STUDY

A radiopaque shadow seen in Left Renal measuring 27X13 mm.
Renal and psas shadows are partially obscured by bowel gases.

SKIAGRAM INTRAVENOUS PYELOGRAPHY:-
5 mts after contrast injection early exposure skiagram shows good nephrographic effect on both side Size shape and location of both kidneys are normal.
No e/o right hydronephrosis seen. No c/o left hydronephrosis.

Bilateral ureters appear normal in caliber.
Contrast filled urinary bladder is clearly outlined margins are regular. No filling defect or indentation seen.

Post voiding skiagram shows no residual contrast .Noother abnormality seen.

IMPRESSION:-LEFT RENAL CALCULUS .

ADVICE UROGRAM FOR BETTER EVALUATION.

Please correlatc clinically.

EVERY TEST HAS ITS OWN INHERENT LIMITATIONS, THEREFORE IN CASE OF ANY DISCREPANCE WITH CLINICAL PROFILE, PLEASE INFORM TO CENTRE IMMEDIATELY.
Timing : Monday to Saturday 08:00 AM to 08:00 PM, Sunday 8:00 AM to 04:00 PM
इमर्जेंसी सेवा 24 घंटे उपलब्ध है !
Not For Medico Legal Purpose

records.summary: This is a typed radiology report for an X-ray IVP study performed on a 34-year-old male on March 21, 2026. The report notes a radiopaque shadow in the left kidney (27x13 mm) and concludes with an impression of left renal calculus, advising a urogram for better evaluation.
key_observations: Patient is a 34-year-old male.
Report describes a radiopaque shadow in the left kidney measuring 27x13 mm.
Findings include normal kidney size, shape, and location; normal bilateral ureters; and a normal contrast-filled bladder.
Final impression: Left renal calculus.
Advice: Urogram for better evaluation.

records.stage_evidence: No contrast agent visible in renal collecting systems in 'PLAIN' view; contrast appears in kidneys, ureters, and bladder in subsequent views

records.summary: Four-panel IVP (Intravenous Pyelogram) series showing the urinary tract at different time intervals after contrast injection: plain film, 5 minutes, 30 minutes, and full bladder view.
key_observations: Four sequential radiographs of the abdomen in PA view
Contrast medium is not visible in the kidneys or ureters in the 'PLAIN' image
Contrast medium is visible in the renal collecting systems in the '5 MIN' image
Contrast medium is visible in the bladder in the 'FULL BLADDER' image
Images are labeled with time intervals and 'CONTROL' markers
